task_completion.description: Identify 'High' priority requirements in the SRS document
task_completion.visual_evidence: ReqView UI is open showing the SRS document with the 'Priority' column visible, and rows with 'High' priority can be seen.

task_completion.description: Locate or display the 'Iteration' custom attribute
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Iteration' column is added to the main document table view, or the 'Iteration' field is visible in the right-hand properties/attributes panel for a selected requirement.
The text 'Sprint 1' is typed into the 'Iteration' field or column for a requirement that has its Priority set to 'High'.

task_completion.description: Ensure all 'High' priority requirements are assigned to 'Sprint 1'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The table view shows multiple rows with 'High' priority, and all of them have 'Sprint 1' in their respective 'Iteration' column.

task_completion.description: Ensure 'Medium' and 'Low' priority requirements are not modified
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rows in the table with 'Medium' or 'Low' priority have an empty 'Iteration' column, or at least do not contain 'Sprint 1'.
The 'File' menu is open with 'Save Project' highlighted/clicked, or the Save icon (floppy disk) in the top toolbar is greyed out, indicating no unsaved changes.

integrity.description: Agent used the ReqView GUI to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Modifications are made within the ReqView application window. There should be no text editors (like VS Code, nano, vim) open editing 'SRS.json' or 'project.json' directly.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal window should not show commands like 'sed', 'awk', 'jq', or python scripts being executed to batch modify the JSON files.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely interacted with the application elements
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible signs of UI interaction such as selected rows highlighted in blue, active text cursors in the 'Iteration' field, or dropdown menus being opened in ReqView.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target priority to look for is 'High'. The custom attribute to modify is 'Iteration' (internal id: 'iteration'). The target value to set is exactly 'Sprint 1'. The document being modified is the SRS document (SRS.json). Ensure that only requirements with 'High' priority have 'Sprint 1' in their Iteration field, and Medium/Low priority requirements remain unchanged.
